items.title: Search for Alice Springs 5yo continues after no sighting overnight

items.title: FOI documents show acting PM sought 'due diligence' on NT administrator

children.content: Months before a furore over his social media posts, documents show the federal government completed background checks on controversial NT Administrator David Connolly.

image.altText: A graphic shows a photo of David Connolly alongside a stack of paperwork.

items.title: What Anzac Day means to those who have served Australia

children.content: Australians taking part in Anzac Day commemorations share stories of sacrifice and bravery, and what today means to them.

image.altText: Female army officer wearing hat and chin strap

items.title: AI ball-tracking technology trialled in Darwin grade cricket

children.content: Darwin's premier women's cricket league has become the first Australian grade competition to use an artificial intelligence umpire review system.

items.title: Industry 'alert but not alarmed' as native weevil develops a taste for cotton

children.content: Farmers and researchers notice a rise in bicoloured weevils feeding on cotton crops in the Northern Territory.

image.altText: Masses of bicoloured weevils on a cotton flower in the NT.

items.title: Indigenous solar projects to tackle diesel shortage concerns and halve power bills

children.content: Two remote Indigenous communities have attracted a combined $11 million in funding for solutions to tackle their fuel security and cost-of-living crises.

image.altText: A photo showing a person walking towards a tractor carrying solar panels surround by fence and car outside the fence

items.title: A fascinating admission reveals truth beneath the chest beating in this war

children.content: It's a ceasefire in name only between Israel and its targets in Lebanon, as the wait continues for more talks between the US and Iran.

articles.title: US allies turning to Japan for defence supplies as Iran war drags on

children.content: Australia's multi-billion-dollar deal with Japan for its future warships could set a template that will help Tokyo rapidly expand defence exports across Asia.

image.altText: A grey frigate is seen in the foreground, sailing in Sydney Harbour, with the bridge to the right of the picture.

items.title: 'Membership to nothing': The multi-million-dollar Wild West of rewards clubs

image.altText: A large 4 wheel drive in a showroom clouded in smoke with dramatic red lighting.

children.content: "Rewards clubs" have exploded in Australia, claiming to have given away hundreds of millions in prizes, but they operate with almost no legal scrutiny.

items.title: An oil shock, a federal budget and the recession we may have to have

children.content: Accompanying the oil shock unfolding in the Strait of Hormuz is a profound change in the way fiscal and monetary policymakers operate. And as the government prepares the budget with the impact of the Iran war still unfolding, a recession may be ahead.

image.altText: A man in a suit from a side profile with his chin jutted out defiantly.

avatar.alt: A man in a suit jacket wearing glasses

items.title: Trump turmoil is political kryptonite in Australia

children.content: As Donald Trump's approval in Australia sinks, Anthony Albanese needs to walk a careful line: keep voters convinced of the merits of the alliance, while avoiding the impression he is being too reverential.

image.altText: Anthony Albanese takes a selfie with a smiling Donald Trump

items.title: Iranian foreign minister heads to Russia for meeting with Vladimir Putin

children.content: Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi will meet Russian President Vladimir Putin during a visit to Moscow later today, the country's ISNA news agency reports. Follow live.

image.altText: An older man with short, grey hair and glasses speaks in front of a grey background.

items.title: Maccas wins battle to set up in 'world's coolest street'

children.content: The fast food giant has been cleared to set up an outlet in the Melbourne suburb of Northcote after successfully challenging a council decision to block its restaurant plans.

image.altText: An architectural rendering of a McDonald's store on High Street in Northcote.

items.title: Two-hour barrier smashed twice in history-making London Marathon

children.content: Kenya's Sabastian Sawe has become the first man to run a marathon in under two hours, winning the London Marathon with a time of 1:59:30.

items.title: Two children believed unaccounted for in Hawkesbury house fire

children.content: Two children are unaccounted for, the ABC understands, after five people were rescued from a house fire in Bowen Mountain on Monday morning in the Hawkesbury region.

items.title: What happens when Kitty Flanagan takes on Anne Edmonds's Bad Company

children.content: Everything Anne Edmonds has done over the last two decades has been leading to Bad Company, an unfettered satire of the theatre world the comedian created, wrote and stars in alongside Kitty Flanagan.

items.title: Why the bedroom is an 'invisible workplace' for women

children.content: There are biological and psychological reasons behind why women experience more disrupted sleep than men, but experts say the mental load is another powerful yet less obvious contributor.

items.title: Wall Street rallies to another record high, ASX set for flat opening

children.content: The S&P 500 rallied to yet another record close as first-quarter earnings continued to beat expectations, but the ASX is priced for a muted opening. Follow the day's events and insights from our business reporters on the ABC News live markets blog.

items.title: When this mum's rental ended, she and her two teenagers moved into their car

items.title: How beer and AI are changing the course of Canberra's economic future

children.content: The capital has long been known as a public service town, but with about two in three workers now employed in the private sector and the population expected to reach nearly 700,000 by 2050, is that reputation — and key economic driver — likely to change?

items.title: Platypus researcher says the species is just 'hanging on' in NSW

items.title: The coastal RSL that traded gambling for a volunteer heartbeat

children.content: When its local RSL club was on the brink of collapse, a community on the NSW South Coast rallied a troop of volunteers to ensure it stayed open.

items.title: Foord stars for Arsenal, Kerr equals record in WSL

children.content: Arsenal's Matildas are scenting more Champions League glory after the holders beat OL Lyonnes 2-1 in the first leg of their semi-final at the Emirates Stadium, while Matildas captain Sam Kerr scored twice to keep Chelsea's slim WSL title ambitions alive in a 4-1 win over Everton.

items.title: Spanish derby descends into chaos after keeper punches opponent in face
